questions.subject: pharmacology
questions.question: Which antibiotic class inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding penicillin-binding proteins?
questions.explanation: Penicillins bind penicillin-binding proteins and block peptidoglycan cross-linking, weakening the bacterial cell wall.

questions.subject: pharmacology
questions.question: Which antibiotic is most strongly associated with tendon injury and should be used cautiously in older adults?
questions.explanation: Fluoroquinolones such as ciprofloxacin are associated with tendonitis and tendon rupture, especially in older adults and those on corticosteroids.
questions.caseScenario: A 67-year-old patient with pneumonia asks why the pharmacist mentioned avoiding strenuous exercise during therapy.

questions.subject: pharmacology
questions.question: Vancomycin is commonly used for serious Gram-positive infections including MRSA.
questions.explanation: Vancomycin is a glycopeptide antibiotic widely used for serious Gram-positive infections, including MRSA.
